extraction.one_line_summary: VLASOV Aleksandr overcame 18-second deficit to DENNIS Rohan with dominant 11.3km climbing TT, winning by over 30 seconds.
tactical_patterns: DENNIS Rohan faded catastrophically in final kilometers despite being TT specialist, losing over 2 minutes to VLASOV.
VLASOV destroyed GESCHKE's time by 30+ seconds despite GESCHKE already beating CARUSO and KRUIJSWIJK comprehensively.
Bike changes at climb base were standard protocol, suggesting flat TT bikes gave negligible advantage on opening kilometers.

rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: First World Tour win came via uphill TT demolition, revealing pure climbing power exceeds perceived capabilities
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: negative: Race leader collapsed on steep gradients despite bike change strategy, exposing climbing TT weakness
rider_signals.GESCHKE Simon: positive: Jumped from 9th to podium with surprise uphill TT performance 33 seconds faster than CARUSO
rider_signals.MÄDER Gino: positive: Only 4 seconds behind GESCHKE despite being 19, signaling elite climbing TT talent
rider_signals.KRUIJSWIJK Steven: neutral: Beat PINOT but finished 30+ seconds behind surprise performers GESCHKE and MÄDER
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: negative: Lost podium spot in final TT despite being best young rider, revealing uphill TT deficiency
